title: migration system silently skips failed multi-statement migrations
description:
LOCATION: src/db.rs:157-163

FINDING: Version 3->4 migration is two ALTER TABLE statements in a single string, applied with sqlx::raw_sql (lines 121-122, 161). If the first ALTER succeeds and the second fails (e.g., column already exists due to a partial previous run), the code catches the error at the call site... but only at the outer await? on line 161. The schema_version is only updated after the loop completes (line 166-169), so a partial migration that returns an error would leave the version un-bumped, causing a retry on next startup. However, there is no transaction wrapping the migration loop, so the first successful ALTER is committed before the second one is attempted.

IMPACT: A crash between the two ALTER TABLE statements in migration v3->v4 leaves the database in a state where did_key column exists but recovery_pubkey does not (or vice versa), schema_version is still 3, and the next startup will attempt to re-apply the whole multi-statement migration, causing the first ALTER to fail with 'duplicate column name'. The server fails to start, and the error message is opaque. A transaction per migration would make this atomic.
close_reason: Fixed: each migration runs in its own transaction with version bump
